sanskrit:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके।
तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः॥
transliteration:
yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ samplutodake
tāvān sarveṣu vedeṣu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ
literal_meaning: Whatever utility is in a well, all that is present in a great lake; so too, all the purposes of the Vedas are already contained for the knower of Brahman.
contextual_meaning: Once the higher-order meaning (Self-knowledge) is realized, the lower-order prescriptions (rituals, rules, status symbols) lose their compulsive grip; the psyche graduates from scattered means to an integrated end.

shadow: Fear of appearing lazy or impious

psychological_state: Post-achievement clarity: the moment when obsessive micro-management gives way to effortless alignment because the core need for identity/security has been internalized.
trigger_scenario: A top-tier coder who once hoarded certificates and frameworks suddenly sees that all problems reduce to first principles; the learning addiction collapses into quiet mastery and she can now choose which tools to use or discard without anxiety.
anchor_text: When you know the Self, every ritual finds its home inside you.
